Disable kitty keyboard `REPORT_EVENT_TYPES` flag (#264)

The kitty keyboard enhancement flag `REPORT_EVENT_TYPES` causes
terminals to emit separate press/release/repeat events. This removes
that flag so only key press events are received.

### Changes

- **`PushKeyboardEnhancementFlags`**: removed `REPORT_EVENT_TYPES` from
the flags bitmask
- **Event loop**: simplified key handler — dropped the `match key.kind`
dispatch since release events no longer arrive
- **`on_keyrelease`**: removed the method (was only used to toggle mouse
capture on Alt release in Simple mode)
- **Stale comment**: removed reference to `on_keyrelease` in the Alt
press handler

```rust
// Before
crossterm::event::PushKeyboardEnhancementFlags(
    KeyboardEnhancementFlags::DISAMBIGUATE_ESCAPE_CODES
        | KeyboardEnhancementFlags::REPORT_EVENT_TYPES   // <-- removed
        | KeyboardEnhancementFlags::REPORT_ALTERNATE_KEYS
        | KeyboardEnhancementFlags::REPORT_ALL_KEYS_AS_ESCAPE_CODES
)
```

smart mouse mode: replace 500ms timer re-enable with focus-gained re-enable (#262)

Smart mouse mode re-enabled capture on a 500ms polling timer, which is
imprecise and wasteful. The correct trigger is terminal focus regained.

## Changes

- **`mouse_state.rs`**: Remove `reenable_after` field and the three
timer methods (`schedule_reenable`, `cancel_reenable`,
`check_reenable_timer`)
- **`app/mod.rs`**:
- `FocusGained` handler: enable mouse capture in Smart mode (unless user
explicitly toggled it off)
- `on_mouse`: drop `schedule_reenable()` calls after disabling on
scroll/above-viewport
  - `on_keypress`: drop `cancel_reenable()` call
  - Poll-timeout handler: remove now-dead Smart mode timer check
- **`settings.rs`**: Update `Smart` variant doc — "re-enabled on any
keypress or when focus is regained"

Add `--tutorial-mode=false` to disable tutorial mode (#266)

Tutorial mode had no way to be turned off once enabled. This adds an
explicit disable flag and a hint in the UI telling users how to use it.

`Option<bool>` with `default_missing_value = "true"` and `require_equals
= true`, enabling:
  - `flyline --tutorial-mode` → enable (backward compatible)
  - `flyline --tutorial-mode=false` → disable

- **`src/app/mod.rs`**: Added a new hint shown on the empty prompt when
tutorial mode is active:
  ```
  💡 Run `flyline --tutorial-mode=false` to disable tutorial mode
  ```

test: add 48 e2e tests for operator edge cases

Cover comma, void, delete, typeof, in, instanceof, nullish
coalescing (??), optional chaining (?.), and exponentiation (**)
operators with end-to-end tests exercising the full parse → compile →
interpret pipeline.

Tests include:
- Comma operator: returns last, side effects, string values
- void: returns undefined, evaluates expression
- delete: obj.prop, non-existent, computed, non-reference
- typeof: all type strings, undeclared (no ReferenceError), symbol
- in: existing/missing props, arrays, prototype chain, throws on primitive
- instanceof: Array, Error, custom class, non-callable throws, negative
- Nullish coalescing: null/undefined → RHS, 0/false/'' → LHS, chained,
  cannot mix with ||/&& without parens, works with parens
- Optional chaining: deep a?.b?.c, null base, computed ?.[],
  optional call ?.(), short-circuit side effects
- Exponentiation: 2**3=8, right-associative, -2**2 SyntaxError,
  x**0=1, fractional exponents
- Combined: typeof===string, in+delete, nullish+void, comma in for

fix: WeakMap/WeakSet conformance — iterable ctors, Symbol keys, key validation

commit.message:
ci: migrate from pip to uv for faster installs (#1431)

## Motivation

Dependency installation is a significant bottleneck in our CI pipelines.
Looking at recent runs of the `pull_request` workflow:

- **CPU integration tests** spend **~200s installing deps** out of ~300s
total (**65%** of wall time)
- **Windows unit tests** spend **50–110s on installs** (38–63% of wall
time)
- Even Linux/macOS unit tests spend ~20s (17–29%)

[uv](https://github.com/astral-sh/uv) is a drop-in replacement for pip
that's 10–100× faster at dependency resolution and installation.
Conservatively, this should cut install times by 5–10×, saving **minutes
per CI run** — especially on the heavier workflows (cpu_tests,
gpu_tests, notebooks).

## Changes

Replace `pip install` with `uv pip install --system` across all 8 CI
workflows that install Python packages:

- Add [`astral-sh/setup-uv@v5`](https://github.com/astral-sh/setup-uv)
step to all jobs
- Replace all `pip install` / `python -m pip install` → `uv pip install
--system`
- Remove now-unnecessary `pip install --upgrade pip` steps
- Use `uv build` instead of `python -m pip install build && python -m
build` in pypi_upload

No changes to `pyproject.toml`, the build backend (still setuptools), or
test configuration. The `--system` flag tells uv to install into the
system Python rather than creating a venv, matching the existing CI
behavior.

## Affected workflows

| Workflow | Install time (before) | % of total |
|---|---|---|
| `call_cpu_tests` | ~200s | 65% |
| `call_gpu_tests` | similar | similar |
| `pull_request` (unit_tests, Windows) | 50–110s | 38–63% |
| `pull_request` (unit_tests, Linux/macOS) | 11–22s | 17–29% |
| `code_quality` | light | — |
| `ci_docs` | moderate | — |
| `ci_credentials` | moderate | — |
| `notebook_tests` | moderate | — |
| `pypi_upload` | light | — |

## What this doesn't do (yet)

This PR only swaps the installer. A natural follow-up would be adding
`uv lock` to generate a `uv.lock` file for fully reproducible CI
environments with pinned transitive dependencies.

Fix heartbeat killed by stale finally block during poll replacement (#39)

* Fix heartbeat killed by stale finally block during poll replacement

When a new pr_monitor_next_step call replaced an old one, the old call's
finally block unconditionally stopped the session heartbeat — killing the
new call's heartbeat and causing an hour+ of silent monitoring.

Add a generation counter to HeartbeatManager so StopGeneration() is a

fix: honour XDG spec in get_app_data_dir — drop expanduser()

Calling expanduser() before is_absolute() caused tilde paths like
~/mydata to expand to an absolute path and pass the check, violating
the XDG Base Directory Specification which requires XDG_DATA_HOME to
be an absolute path (not tilde-prefixed). Removing the expanduser()
call ensures only paths already starting with / are used.

Implement IMPALA ResNet with CoordConv and multi-head attention (#180)

* Add IMPALA ResNet with CoordConv and spatial attention pooling

New visual encoder architecture for full-screen RGB observation:

Architecture:
- ResBlock: Pre-activation residual block (ReLU→Conv→ReLU→Conv + skip)
- ImpalaResNet: 3 residual stacks (5→16→32→32) with CoordConv buffers
  and spatial self-attention pooling, producing a 256-dim feature vector
- SpatialAttentionPool: 1×1 conv Q/K/V projections, scaled dot-product
  attention over all spatial positions, weighted-sum pooling
- ImpalaCombinedExtractor: ImpalaResNet + entity attention + info = 335-dim
- ImpalaSharedAgent / ImpalaMultiHeadAgent: New Network subclasses with
  forward_with_attention() for visualization

All spatial dimensions are dynamic (works for both 168×240 cropped and
176×256 full overscan modes). CoordConv channels registered as buffers.

Observation:
- New obs_kind='full-rgb': HUD trimmed, RGB (no grayscale), no viewport
  crop, frame_stack=1. Output shape: (3, H, W) normalized [0,1].

Config:
- New model-kinds in triforce.yaml: impala-shared, impala-multihead

Debugger:
- ATTENTION overlay flag in OverlayFlags enum
- Attention heatmap checkbox in View menu
- JET colormap heatmap overlay on gameplay area
- Attention weights fetched from model via forward_with_attention()

Tests:
- 28 new tests covering ResBlock, SpatialAttentionPool, ImpalaResNet,
  ImpalaSharedAgent, ImpalaMultiHeadAgent, registration, save/load,
  both screen sizes, CoordConv buffer properties

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Auto-detect obs_kind from saved models in evaluate/diagnose/debug

Add infer_obs_kind() to observation_wrapper.py that determines the
observation kind (viewport/full-rgb/gameplay) and frame_stack from
the saved observation space shape. This removes the need for explicit
--obs-kind parameters when loading existing models.

- evaluate.py: uses infer_obs_kind(network.observation_space)
- diagnose.py: uses infer_obs_kind(metadata['obs_space']) at all 3 sites
- environment_bridge.py: infers from model metadata, returns obs_space
- train.py: infers from --load model when available, auto-detects from
  model_kind name for new training (impala-* defaults to full-rgb)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix IMPALA OOM: auto-increase minibatches for spatial attention

The 630-token spatial attention matrix (630×630 × batch) causes OOM
with the default 4 minibatches (minibatch_size=1024). Add
recommended_minibatches=16 to IMPALA agents, which PPO respects
unless explicitly overridden. This reduces minibatch size to 256,
cutting attention memory from ~1.9GB to ~0.5GB per minibatch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add attention dropout to prevent attention collapse

SpatialAttentionPool now applies dropout (p=0.1) to the attention
weights during training. This prevents the model from fixating on a
single spatial position (e.g., the CoordConv corner) and encourages
it to learn content-dependent attention patterns.

Note: this changes the model architecture, so existing checkpoints
are not compatible — training must restart from scratch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log attention entropy and top-1 weight to tensorboard

Adds 'attention/entropy' and 'attention/top1_weight' metrics for
IMPALA models. Entropy near ln(N) means uniform attention (healthy),
near 0 means collapsed to one position (bad). Top-1 weight directly
shows the fraction of attention on the most-attended position.

fix: Proxy+Reflect round-trip conformance for all 13 traps

Bug fixes:
- proxy_set_prototype_of default path now converts the proto JsValue
  into a proper JsObject instead of discarding it and creating an empty
  one (was losing all prototype properties)
- target_prototype_value now handles JsObject targets that have a
  prototype via Rc<RefCell<JsObject>>, converting them to a PlainObject
  snapshot so invariant checks using same_value work correctly
- Fix doc comment on ProxyHandler example (closure had wrong arity)

New Reflect bridge functions:

  to match proxy's JsValue-based prototype API
- reflect_set_prototype_of_value: accepts JsValue, converts to
  Option<Rc<RefCell<JsObject>>> internally

  match proxy_own_keys return type

Test suite (48 tests in proxy_reflect.rs):
- Default (no-trap) round-trip for get, set, has, deleteProperty,
  ownKeys, getPrototypeOf, setPrototypeOf, defineProperty,
  getOwnPropertyDescriptor, isExtensible, preventExtensions
- Receiver forwarding for get/set
- apply/construct through traps matching Reflect.apply/construct
- Nested Proxy composition (get, has, set, ownKeys through inner+outer)
- All 13 traps on revoked proxy throw TypeError

- Bridge function edge cases (non-extensible, primitive rejection)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: property enumeration order conformance (ES §10.1.11)

Fixes property key enumeration to follow the ECMAScript specification:
integer indices ascending, then string keys in insertion order, then
symbol keys in insertion order.

Changes:
- PropertyMap::spec_insert_pos: symbol keys are now placed after all
  non-symbol string keys; non-symbol string keys are inserted before
  any trailing symbol keys rather than always appending at the end.
- JsObject::own_property_keys: applies sort_keys_spec_order() to
  produce spec-compliant key ordering in both fast and slow mode.
- Added sort_keys_spec_order() helper to js_object.rs that partitions
  keys into integer indices (sorted ascending), strings (preserving
  relative insertion order), and symbols (preserving relative order).

All downstream consumers (Object.keys/values/entries,
Object.getOwnPropertyNames, Object.getOwnPropertySymbols,
Reflect.ownKeys, Proxy ownKeys, for-in, JSON.stringify) now inherit
the corrected order.

42 new unit tests covering:
- Integer index sorting (ascending, sparse, large values)
- String key insertion order preservation
- Symbol keys after strings
- Mixed integer+string+symbol ordering
- Non-enumerable filtering in Object.keys vs getOwnPropertyNames
- Object.values/entries matching key order
- Reflect.ownKeys including non-enumerable keys
- Proxy ownKeys trap order preservation
- JSON.stringify key ordering

Fix remaining String edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set correct property descriptors on globalThis

Per ECMAScript §19, global object properties have specific attribute
requirements:

- Value properties (undefined, NaN, Infinity): non-writable,
  non-enumerable, non-configurable.
- Function properties (eval, isFinite, isNaN, parseFloat, parseInt,
  decodeURI, encodeURI, decodeURIComponent, encodeURIComponent):
  writable, non-enumerable, configurable.
- Constructor properties (Object, Function, Array, etc.) and namespace
  objects (Math, JSON, Reflect): writable, non-enumerable, configurable.
- globalThis itself: writable, non-enumerable, configurable.

Previously all globalThis properties were inserted with default
attributes (writable | enumerable | configurable) because the
PropertyMap copy loop used plain insert(). Now each property gets the
correct descriptor via insert_with_attrs().

Adds 61 end-to-end tests verifying:
- Descriptor attributes for value, function, and constructor properties
- globalThis === this identity
- typeof on undeclared variable yields 'undefined'
- let/const declarations not visible on globalThis
- Non-enumerability (not in Object.keys)
- hasOwnProperty checks
